mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates above 4.5% compress equity valuation multiples by raising the discount rate for future corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5% signals labor market slack that reduces consumer spending growth and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate a collapsing housing demand cycle that reduces wealth effects and consumer credit expansion, producing negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55% during co-declining periods.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index above 110 reflects robust manufacturing and utility output that supports near-term corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO values above 110 are followed by negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.3% monthly threshold forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing borrowing costs and compressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the inflation threshold shows no incremental predictive value over baseline models.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts household income and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The co-movement of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to generate positive 60-day returns, with negative returns occurring in >55% of aligned observations.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects sustained aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth periods yield negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal exhibits a rolling Rank IC below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline below 0.1% signals supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing activity that dampens near-term earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when industrial production growth falls below the 0.1% threshold, or the signal shows no statistical edge in walk-forward validation.
